extraction.one_line_summary: CAVENDISH Mark won the sprint after MØRKØV Michael piloted him through the decisive right-hander at 1200m, beating PHILIPSEN Jasper in a two-up finish.
tactical_patterns: Israel Premier Tech controlled the compression into the 1200m right-hander, dictating sprint positioning when highway narrowed into corner.
Groupama-FDJ and BikeExchange committed leadout trains too early at 10km in headwind-to-crosswind transition, burned before final corner.
Quick-Step kept CAVENDISH hidden until emerging from right-hander, avoiding early wind exposure while rivals showed themselves at 10km.
BENNETT Sam boxed at decisive corner despite early positioning, suggesting sprint trains fragment predictably when roads compress at 1200m markers.

rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: executed perfect late emergence after MØRKØV positioning, still has elite acceleration when protected through technical finishes
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: well-positioned through corner and matched CAVENDISH in two-up sprint, but lacks final 50m punch against elite sprinters
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: trapped despite team positioning efforts, lacks instinct or power to force positioning through compressed technical corners
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: neutral: third place suggests good positioning but not contesting when CAVENDISH and PHILIPSEN separated themselves
rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: negative: unhappy finish and fifth place indicates positioning error or lack of power after his team committed early
rider_signals.MØRKØV Michael: positive: piloted CAVENDISH through chaos of 1200m corner, remains unmatched at delivering sprinters through technical finishes
